models/staging/stg_customers.yml.purpose: Maintains cleaned customer information for business relationship management, with each row representing a unique customer.

models/staging/stg_products.yml.purpose: Manages product information for food and drink items available for ordering, with each row representing a unique product.

macros/cents_to_dollars.sql.purpose: Converts monetary values from cents to dollars format for consistent financial reporting across different database platforms.

models/marts/orders.sql.purpose: This model creates a comprehensive orders mart by enriching staged order data with summarized item-level metrics and customer ordering sequence information. It provides key business insights including order costs, item counts, food/drink categorization, and customer order history for downstream analytics and reporting.

models/marts/order_items.yml.purpose: This file defines the order items mart, which contains detailed information about individual items within orders, including their revenue, cost, and categorization as food or drink items. It also includes business logic for calculating various revenue metrics and testing supply cost calculations.

models/marts/customers.yml.purpose: This file defines a customer data mart that aggregates lifetime spending, order counts, and customer segmentation metrics. It enables downstream reporting on customer value and behavior through semantic modeling and pre-defined metrics.

macros/generate_schema_name.sql.purpose: Defines naming conventions for database schemas to organize data assets according to environment and resource type for better data governance.

models/marts/order_items.sql.purpose: This mart model creates a comprehensive view of order items by joining staging tables for orders, products, and supplies to calculate total supply costs per product and enrich order item data with product details and order timestamps.

models/marts/customers.sql.purpose: Creates a comprehensive customer analytics dataset that enables customer segmentation and lifetime value analysis by aggregating order history with customer demographics.

models/marts/supplies.yml.purpose: Defines the supplies semantic model to enable business metrics analysis by supply characteristics like cost and perishability.

models/staging/stg_orders.yml.purpose: Maintains clean and validated order transaction data with financial consistency checks for accurate business reporting.

models/staging/stg_supplies.yml.purpose: Tracks supply expense costs for business operations, with each row representing a supply cost entry that may change over time.

models/staging/stg_orders.sql.purpose: This staging model transforms raw e-commerce order data by renaming fields, converting cent values to dollar amounts, and truncating timestamps to daily granularity. It serves as a standardized intermediate layer for downstream analytics and reporting on order transactions.

models/staging/stg_products.sql.purpose: This staging model transforms raw product data from the e-commerce system into a standardized format suitable for analytics. It cleans and renames fields, converts price units from cents to dollars, and adds boolean flags to categorize products as food or drink items.

models/marts/orders.yml.purpose: This file defines a data mart for order analytics, containing key order details like totals, timestamps, and item breakdowns. It supports downstream reporting through semantic models, metrics, and saved queries focused on order trends and customer behavior.

models/marts/locations.yml.purpose: Establishes location-based dimensions and metrics for business intelligence reporting, particularly focusing on tax rate analysis across different locations.

models/staging/stg_order_items.yml.purpose: Provides cleaned and validated individual food and drink item data to support order-level business analysis and reporting.

models/marts/locations.sql.purpose: Provides location data for business sites, transforming raw location information into a standardized format for reporting and analysis.

models/marts/products.yml.purpose: Defines the product dimension for semantic modeling, enabling consistent product categorization and pricing analysis across business metrics.

models/marts/metricflow_time_spine.sql.purpose: Generates a comprehensive date spine for time-series analysis and metric calculations across business reporting periods.

models/staging/stg_supplies.sql.purpose: This staging model transforms raw supply data from the e-commerce system into a standardized format for downstream analytics. It cleans and renames fields, generates surrogate keys, and converts cost values from cents to dollars while preserving all original supply information.

models/staging/stg_locations.sql.purpose: This staging model transforms raw store data into a standardized format by renaming fields and extracting date components. It serves as an intermediate layer between raw e-commerce store data and downstream analytics models.

models/staging/stg_customers.sql.purpose: This staging model transforms raw customer data by renaming fields to standardized column names. It selects customer identifiers and names from the source system for downstream consumption.

models/staging/stg_order_items.sql.purpose: This staging model transforms raw e-commerce item data into a standardized format by renaming and selecting key fields such as order item ID, order ID, and product ID.

models/staging/stg_locations.yml.purpose: Provides a cleaned and standardized list of open locations for downstream analytics, ensuring each location is uniquely identified and properly formatted.

models/marts/products.sql.purpose: Provides a cleaned and standardized view of product data for business reporting and analysis.

models/marts/supplies.sql.purpose: Delivers a unified view of supply chain data to support inventory and supplier management decisions.

models/staging/__sources.yml.purpose: Configures the connection to raw e-commerce data sources to enable downstream data transformation and analysis.

dbt_project.yml.purpose: This is the main configuration file for a dbt project named 'jaffle_shop' that defines project metadata, resource paths, and model configurations. It specifies database connection settings, directory structures, and materialization strategies for different model types.

.pre-commit-config.yaml.purpose: This configuration file defines pre-commit hooks that automatically validate and format code before commits are made, ensuring code quality and consistency across the repository.

Taskfile.yml.purpose: Defines automated workflows for environment setup, data generation, and database operations to streamline development processes.

Customer Relationship Management.description: Manages customer data, lifetime value analysis, segmentation, and relationship tracking for business intelligence.

Product & Menu Management.description: Handles product catalog data, pricing, food/drink categorization, and supply chain cost tracking for inventory and menu operations.

Order Processing & Analytics.description: Processes order transactions, item-level details, revenue calculations, and provides comprehensive order analytics for business reporting.

Location & Tax Management.description: Manages store location data, site information, and tax rate analysis for geographic business intelligence.

Data Infrastructure & Governance.description: Provides foundational data infrastructure including time dimensions, schema management, currency conversion, and project configuration.

business_logic_hotspots.purpose: Maintains cleaned customer information for business relationship management, with each row representing a unique customer.
Manages product information for food and drink items available for ordering, with each row representing a unique product.
Converts monetary values from cents to dollars format for consistent financial reporting across different database platforms.

This model creates a comprehensive orders mart by enriching staged order data with summarized item-level metrics and customer ordering sequence information. It provides key business insights including order costs, item counts, food/drink categorization, and customer order history for downstream analytics and reporting.

This file defines the order items mart, which contains detailed information about individual items within orders, including their revenue, cost, and categorization as food or drink items. It also includes business logic for calculating various revenue metrics and testing supply cost calculations.

This file defines a customer data mart that aggregates lifetime spending, order counts, and customer segmentation metrics. It enables downstream reporting on customer value and behavior through semantic modeling and pre-defined metrics.
Defines naming conventions for database schemas to organize data assets according to environment and resource type for better data governance.

This mart model creates a comprehensive view of order items by joining staging tables for orders, products, and supplies to calculate total supply costs per product and enrich order item data with product details and order timestamps.
Creates a comprehensive customer analytics dataset that enables customer segmentation and lifetime value analysis by aggregating order history with customer demographics.
Defines the supplies semantic model to enable business metrics analysis by supply characteristics like cost and perishability.

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This staging model transforms raw product data from the e-commerce system into a standardized format suitable for analytics. It cleans and renames fields, converts price units from cents to dollars, and adds boolean flags to categorize products as food or drink items.

supporting_evidence.description: Field renaming (sku→product_id, name→product_name), price conversion via {{ cents_to_dollars('price') }}, and boolean logic for is_food_item/is_drink_item
Transformation dbt_model reads source.ecom.raw_products and writes model.stg_products
Import reference to source

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This staging model transforms raw e-commerce order data by renaming fields, converting cent values to dollar amounts, and truncating timestamps to daily granularity. It serves as a standardized intermediate layer for downstream analytics and reporting on order transactions.

supporting_evidence.description: Field renaming (id→order_id, store_id→location_id, customer→customer_id) and currency conversion using {{ cents_to_dollars() }} macro
Timestamp truncation using {{ dbt.date_trunc('day','ordered_at') }}
Transformation dbt_model reads source.ecom.raw_orders and writes model.stg_orders

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This staging model transforms raw supply data from the e-commerce system into a standardized format for downstream analytics. It cleans and renames fields, generates surrogate keys, and converts cost values from cents to dollars while preserving all original supply information.

supporting_evidence.description: dbt_utils.generate_surrogate_key(['id', 'sku']) creates supply_uuid
cents_to_dollars('cost') converts cost values
source('ecom', 'raw_supplies') references raw supply data

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This mart model creates a comprehensive view of order items by joining staging tables for orders, products, and supplies to calculate total supply costs per product and enrich order item data with product details and order timestamps.

supporting_evidence.description: Aggregates supply costs by product_id in order_supplies_summary CTE
Joins multiple staging tables to create enriched order item records with product details and supply costs
dbt mart model pattern with multiple CTEs for data transformation

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This model creates a comprehensive orders mart by enriching staged order data with summarized item-level metrics and customer ordering sequence information. It provides key business insights including order costs, item counts, food/drink categorization, and customer order history for downstream analytics and reporting.

supporting_evidence.description: order_items_summary CTE computes supply cost, product price, and item type counts
compute_booleans CTE joins order data with item summaries and derives food/drink order flags
customer_order_count CTE calculates customer order sequence using window function

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This staging model transforms raw store data into a standardized format by renaming fields and extracting date components. It serves as an intermediate layer between raw e-commerce store data and downstream analytics models.

supporting_evidence.description: Field renaming operations: id→location_id, name→location_name, and timestamp truncation for opened_date
Source reference to ecom.raw_stores dataset
Transformation dbt_model reads source.ecom.raw_stores and writes model.stg_locations

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This staging model transforms raw customer data by renaming fields to standardized column names. It selects customer identifiers and names from the source system for downstream consumption.

supporting_evidence.description: id as customer_id, name as customer_name
Transformation dbt_model reads source.ecom.raw_customers and writes model.stg_customers
Import reference to source

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This staging model transforms raw e-commerce item data into a standardized format by renaming and selecting key fields such as order item ID, order ID, and product ID.

supporting_evidence.description: Field renaming logic mapping 'id' to 'order_item_id', 'sku' to 'product_id'
Source reference to 'ecom.raw_items' indicates input dataset
Transformation dbt_model reads source.ecom.raw_items and writes model.stg_order_items

semantic_hotspots.purpose: Creates a comprehensive customer analytics dataset that enables customer segmentation and lifetime value analysis by aggregating order history with customer demographics.

supporting_evidence.description: Calculates lifetime metrics including spend, order count, and repeat buyer status
Segments customers into new vs returning types for marketing purposes
Transformation dbt_model reads model.stg_customers, model.orders and writes model.customers

semantic_hotspots.purpose: Provides location data for business sites, transforming raw location information into a standardized format for reporting and analysis.

supporting_evidence.description: SQL query selects all data from stg_locations reference, indicating location data transformation
Simple SELECT * transformation suggests standardization of location data for downstream use
Transformation dbt_model reads model.stg_locations and writes model.locations
